commit.message:
fix(image): pre-compute file size with stat before CDI polling loop

wc -c reads every byte of the file on macOS, which burned seconds of
CDI's countdown window between detecting Initialized and the first data
byte arriving. Replace with stat (inode read, instant on both Linux and
macOS) and move the computation before the polling loop so curl starts
streaming with zero additional delay once CDI is ready.

Also remove the redundant post-loop Active re-check: the polling loop
already checks for Active on every iteration including the last one, so
the extra curl round-trip was pure waste right before the upload.

Update README with a CDI timing-sensitivity explanation, RetryLimitExceeded
troubleshooting steps, and API verification commands. Update AGENTS.md
upload flow description and add Do-not rules for stat/wc-c and IMAGE_SIZE
placement.

commit.message:
Warn user when uploaded document may exceed model's context window (#1074)

Previously, uploading a large document and sending it would fail hard
with a cryptic LLM API error. There was no upfront check or user-facing
hint before the request was made.

## Changes

- **`AppChat.jsx`** — Adds `fileTokenWarning` state + `useEffect` that
estimates document token count (`chars / 4` heuristic) on file selection
and compares against the selected model's `tokenLimit`. Triggers when
estimated tokens exceed 80% of the context window (leaves headroom for
system prompt, user message, and response). Handles single and
multi-file uploads.

- **`ChatInput.jsx`** — Accepts `fileTokenWarning` prop and renders an
amber inline banner (⚠️) between the attached files list and the text
input. Non-blocking — user can still submit. Disappears when the file is
removed or a model with a larger context window is selected.

- **`shared/i18n/en.json` + `de.json`** — Adds `errors.documentTooLarge`
with `{{estimatedTokens}}` and `{{tokenLimit}}` interpolation.

## Example warning

When a 500KB PDF is attached to a model with a 32K token limit:

> ⚠️ The document is large (~125,000 estimated tokens) and may exceed
the model's context window of 32,000 tokens. The request may fail.
Consider using a shorter document or selecting a model with a larger
context window.

commit.message:
[main] Source code updates from dotnet/msbuild (#5183)

[main] Source code updates from dotnet/msbuild


 - Add MSBuild apphost installer changes

Adds SDK layout and installer support for the MSBuild apphost,
following the same pattern as the Roslyn apphost changes (#3180).

Changes:
- Define _MSBuildAppHost item in Directory.Build.props
- Add CreateMSBuildAppHost target in GenerateLayout.targets
  (CreateAppHost, COM .tlb copy on Windows, codesign on macOS)
- Add MSBuild apphost chmod in ChmodPublishDir and Crossgen targets
- Add macOS signing config for MSBuild apphost in Signing.props
- Add msbuild-entitlements.plist for macOS codesigning
- Add Microsoft.Build.Framework.tlb binary for Windows COM support
- Allow .tlb in allowed-vmr-binaries.txt

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

 - Fix BuildEnvironmentHelper to not use apphost when running via dotnet

TryFromMSBuildAppHost and TryFromAppContextBaseDirectory now check
IsRunningInMSBuildExe before using the MSBuild apphost as the current
MSBuild executable. Without this, worker nodes get launched via the
apphost, changing Environment.ProcessPath to the SDK directory where
dotnet is not present, breaking Process.Start("dotnet") resolution
in tasks like VSTestTask.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

 - Generate Microsoft.Build.Framework.tlb at build time instead of checking in binary

- Remove checked-in .tlb binary from sdk Layout; generate at build time
  via tlbexp.exe during net472 build (CreateTypeLib target)
- Unified tlbexp.exe resolution: registry-based lookup with hardcoded
  fallback, works on both desktop and Core MSBuild (no MSBuildRuntimeType branching)
- Generate both x86 and x64 type libraries in all builds
  (the .tlb ships in both Visual Studio and the .NET SDK on Windows)
- Enable CreateTlb in VMR Windows builds (DotNetBuildSourceOnly != true)
- Include .tlb in Microsoft.Build.Framework NuGet package (lib/net472/)
- SDK layout copies .tlb from NuGet package cache with Exists() guard

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

 - Fix VSTestTask to use DOTNET_HOST_PATH and revert BuildEnvironmentHelper workaround

VSTestTask: Use DOTNET_HOST_PATH environment variable (set by the SDK) to
resolve the dotnet host path, with PATH search as fallback. This is the
proper fix for the 'dotnet not found' error that occurs when MSBuild runs
via the apphost and Environment.ProcessPath changes to the SDK directory.

BuildEnvironmentHelper: Revert the IsRunningInMSBuildExe guard in
TryFromMSBuildAppHost and the context-aware logic in

commit.message:
docs(examples): add deployment examples for docker-compose, kubernetes, and systemd (#1693)

Manael lacks reference deployment configurations, making it harder for
users to adopt best-practice architectures (especially with a caching
frontend). Adds an `examples/` directory with ready-to-use
configurations for three environments.

## docker-compose (`examples/docker-compose/`)
- **`compose.yaml`**: Nginx (port 80) fronting Manael (port 8080)
- **`nginx.conf`**: Nginx cache with an explicit `proxy_cache_key` built
from only relevant query params — prevents cache fragmentation from
parameter ordering or tracking params like `utm_source`:
  ```nginx
proxy_cache_key
"$scheme$proxy_host$uri?w=$arg_w&h=$arg_h&q=$arg_q&fit=$arg_fit";
  ```
- **`.env.example`**: All supported env vars with inline docs

## Kubernetes (`examples/kubernetes/`)
- **`deployment.yaml`**: 2-replica deployment with resource limits,
liveness/readiness probes on `/_health`, and a hardened security context
(non-root, read-only root filesystem, all capabilities dropped, writable
`/tmp` via `emptyDir`)
- **`service.yaml`**: ClusterIP service for cluster-internal routing

## systemd (`examples/systemd/`)
- **`manael.service`**: Unit file with `Restart=on-failure`, non-root
`manael` user, and hardening directives (`NoNewPrivileges`,
`ProtectSystem=strict`, `PrivateTmp`, etc.)
- **`manael.env`**: All supported env vars, loaded via
`EnvironmentFile=`

commit.message:
fix: rate limit not detected in idle handler and pending-message paths

Three bugs caused rate limits to be silently swallowed, leaving the loop
to enter SLEEPING state instead of showing the rate limit banner.

Bug 1 – Launcher message ordering: If the SDK emits rate_limit_event
AFTER the result message, the previous fix (checking rateLimitText when
processing result) didn't fire. Fix: in the rate_limit_event handler,
immediately set isError=true and errorMessage=rateLimitText so the rate
limit text is always in result.error regardless of message order.

Bug 2 – Idle handler paths (Id / Superego / Ego): All three roles silently
swallowed rate-limit errors — Id.generateDrives() returned [], Superego
rejected all proposals, Ego.respondToMessage() returned null — causing
IdleHandler to return 'no_goals'/'all_rejected' and the orchestrator to
call enterSleep() instead of backing off.

Fix: add RateLimitError and isRateLimitText() helpers. Id, Superego, and
Ego now throw RateLimitError when result.error contains rate limit text.
The orchestrator re-throws it from the respondToMessage catch block. A
top-level try/catch in runLoop() catches RateLimitError from any path
(cycle, idle handler, message response) and delegates to the new
applyRateLimitBackoff() helper, which was extracted from the existing

commit.message:
feat: numeric route ID compression for O(1) dispatch (#1313)

Add NumericRouteTable — a flat array-indexed dispatch table that
assigns deterministic sequential IDs to all routes (sorted by key
for stability across restarts):

- Build(): collects routes, sorts by key, assigns IDs 0..N-1
- TryLookup(int routeId): O(1) array index, zero string comparison
- ParseRouteId(): branchless ASCII digit parser that stops at
  first non-digit character

Dispatch fast path in BareMetalWebServer.RequestHandler:
- If path starts with /<digit>, parse route ID and dispatch via
  array lookup before the jump table/prefix router stages
- Falls back to existing routing for non-numeric paths
- Full auth/logging integration on the numeric path

Metadata export endpoint: GET /bmw/routes returns JSON array of
RouteId → Verb → Path mappings for client library integration.

25 new tests covering: table building, deterministic ID assignment,
lookup (valid/invalid/out-of-range/empty), rebuild, parser
(digits, non-digits, trailing slash/query/space, edge cases).

Updated RegisterMonitoringRoutes route count assertion (5→6).

Closes #1311

commit.message:
perf: property cache, cheap_clone, Smi fast-paths for bitwise/div/mod (#448)

* perf(property_map): add inline name cache for fast repeated lookups

Add a 4-entry FNV-1a hash-based inline cache to PropertyMap that sits in
front of the HashMap index. On repeated lookups of the same property names
(the common case in tight JS loops), the cache returns the slot index via
a cheap linear scan of 4 hash entries, avoiding the full SipHash + probe
overhead of HashMap::get.

The cache uses Cell-based interior mutability so read-only (&self) methods
like get(), contains_key(), and get_with_attrs() can populate the cache
transparently. The cache is automatically invalidated on remove() since
swap-remove changes slot indices.

PartialEq is now implemented manually to exclude cache state from equality
comparisons.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf(interpreter): reduce JsValue clone overhead in hot dispatch paths

- Add JsValue::cheap_clone() that does bitwise copy for scalar variants
  (Smi, HeapNumber, Boolean, Undefined, Null, etc.) without going
  through the derived Clone which may touch Rc refcounts unnecessarily.

- Add InterpreterFrame::copy_reg() for Mov opcode, avoiding the
  intermediate read_reg/write_reg borrow dance and skipping the copy
  when src == dst.

- Switch Ldar, Star, Mov, Add, Sub, Mul slow paths, and TestEqual/
  TestNotEqual to use cheap_clone() instead of clone().

- Add Smi fast paths to Div, Mod, Exp, BitwiseOr, BitwiseXor,
  BitwiseAnd, ShiftLeft, ShiftRight, and ShiftRightLogical handlers.
  These return early without cloning the register value when both
  operands are Smi (Copy semantics via pattern match).

- Add #[inline] to JsValue::to_number() (hot conversion path).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add String.prototype.trimLeft/trimRight legacy aliases

Add trimLeft as alias for trimStart and trimRight as alias for trimEnd
per ECMAScript Annex B §B.2.3.1 and §B.2.3.2.

All other conformance items (optional chaining, nullish coalescing,
logical assignment operators, numeric separators, globalThis,
Array.prototype.flat/flatMap, Object.hasOwn, Error.cause, and at()
on String/Array/TypedArray) were already implemented.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: BMW protocol descriptor and SDK generator (#1325) (#1336)

BmwProtocolDescriptor.cs — deterministic protocol descriptor:
- Enumerates all registered routes, computes opcodes via (method<<11)|route
- RouteDescriptor: name, opcode, method, routeOrdinal, entity, params, permission
- EntityDescriptor: groups all opcodes for an entity slug
- Lookup tables: by name, by opcode, by entity
- ToJson(): cached JSON serialization of full protocol spec
- GenerateJsSdk(): virtual SDK with WebSocket binary transport
- GenerateCliReference(): CLI command mappings

Endpoints registered in EnsureBinaryTransport():
- GET /bmw/protocol — full protocol descriptor JSON
- GET /bmw/sdk.js — auto-generated JS SDK module
- GET /bmw/cli — CLI command reference

The descriptor is the single shared contract between server and all clients.
Opcode generation is deterministic; route ordinals are stable across restarts.

Tests: 17 new tests covering opcode computation, entity grouping, parameter
extraction, JSON validity, transport constants, SDK generation, CLI output,
caching, and edge cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
